---
title: "Cluster Computing"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
execute:
  echo: false
  fig-width: 6
  fig-asp: 0.6
  out-width: "90%"
---



## Overview

In this, we will introduce methods for Cluster Computing.

```{r, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(microbenchmark)
```

::: {.panel-tabset .panel-pills}

### Slide Deck: Using the HPC

[![](./slides/topic_cluster/preview_cluster.png){width=70%}](./slides/topic_cluster/s_intro.pdf)

**Guest lecture, Keven Haynes**  
[Download slides (PDF)](./slides/topic_cluster/s_intro.pdf)


***

### Slide Deck: Project organization on the cluster

[![](./slides/topic_cluster/preview_cluster_org.png){width=70%}](./slides/topic_cluster/s_cluster_org.pdf)

**Project organization**  
[Download slides (PDF)](./slides/topic_cluster/s_cluster_org.pdf)

- [Download cluster_project.zip](./slides/topic_cluster/s_cluster_org.pdf)


***

### Lab: Power simulation on the cluster

You have two files: `power_sim.R` (estimates t-test power via simulation across four sample sizes) and `power_sim.sh` (submits it to the cluster). The `doLocal` flag in `power_sim.R` runs scenario 1 locally; on the cluster the scenario is passed as a command-line argument.



#### Part 0: Run locally

Run `power_sim.R` in RStudio and confirm it completes without errors and writes a `.RDA` file to `results/`.

---

#### Part 1: Transfer and submit a single job

Copy the files to your scratch directory, then log in:

```bash
scp power_sim.R power_sim.sh <user>@clogin01.sph.emory.edu:/scratch/BIOS731/<your_folder>/
ssh <user>@clogin01.sph.emory.edu
cd ../../scratch/BIOS731/<your_folder>
mkdir results
```

Submit and monitor:

```bash
sbatch power_sim.sh
squeue -u <user>
```

Check `power_sim.out` for the expected message and `power_sim.err` for errors.


---

#### Part 2: Convert to a job array

Use `vim` to edit `power_sim.sh`. Make two changes: add `--array=1-4` and replace the hard-coded `JOBID=1` with `$SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID`.


```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --array=1-4
#SBATCH --job-name=power_sim
#SBATCH --partition=wrobel
#SBATCH --output=power_sim_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=power_sim_%a.err

module purge
module load R

JOBID=$SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID
Rscript power_sim.R $JOBID
```

Submit and verify that four `.RDA` files appear in `results/`.

---

#### Part 3: Modify the simulation

Use `vim power_sim.R` to make one of the following changes, then resubmit the array.

**Option A** — change `nsim` from 1000 to 2000. Do estimates change?

**Option B** — change `alpha` from 0.05 to 0.01. How does power change?

***
:::
